what_it_does: Design document specifying a brain-agnostic address-to-territory composer (territory_for / territory_prose) that gathers identity, context, chats, H1.2 relations, and degrade notes for arbitrary scheme addresses, along with a bridge handoff plan and live-store refinements.
observations: Defines API territory_for(address, suite=None, store=None) returning a dict with legs: address, scheme, identity, identity_kind, context_items, chats, relations, notes, legs_present.
Defines territory_prose as the renderer of the [Operator context — what Tim is pointing at] block and the bridge-facing drop-in replacement.
Composes existing resolvers (cognition.resolve_address, suite.address_help/context_at/chats_at, cc_board.relations) rather than forking them.
9 resolvable schemes go through cognition.resolve_address; 6 non-resolvable schemes degrade to a noted-absent leg; ui:// gets a richer three-leg composer; H1.2 graph edges apply to any scheme.

Degrade contract distinguishes three failure cases: malformed/non-address raises; non-resolvable scheme returns partial with note; well-formed resolvable-but-nonexistent record is per-leg guarded so it becomes a noted-absent leg.
territory_prose is specified to never raise, preserving the existing bridge wire contract.
Verification plan emphasizes a degrade matrix, not just happy path.
Proposes a one-line bridge handoff replacing _claude_stream's inline ui://-only composer; fork does not edit bridge.py.
Refinements cover projection:select CustomEvent, detail.address vs detail.record, code:// vs run:// corpus records, and a correction verified against the live store.
File carries a trust/author/status header and marks build as HELD by host directive while also stating BUILT + VERIFIED-BY-USE.
standouts: The explicit degrade matrix and per-leg guard contract designed to prevent regressing the bridge wire's fail-soft behavior.
Brain-agnostic composition: SCALE/rung decides FOCUS vs PANEL at handoff, not inside the composer.
Live-store verification corrected an earlier code:// scope/blast-radius guess into a corpus-record dereference path.
The tension between 'BUILT + VERIFIED-BY-USE' and 'BUILD HELD by host directive'.
conventions: Markdown design doc with a structured trust/author/status header at the top.
References specific files and line ranges for provenance.
Uses project-specific terms: fork-owned, file-disjoint, meet-at-the-address, reuse-don't-parallel.
Stars mark key/refinement items; design-only items are explicitly labeled as finding-phase.
Status metadata mixes design, build, and verification state.
concerns: The status header says both BUILT+VERIFIED-BY-USE and BUILD HELD, which is locally confusing about readiness.
The degrade contract depends on per-leg try/except behavior that is described but not shown in this file.
Several forward-referenced pieces (FE listener, projection:select consumer, bridge owner patch application) live outside this file.
Gating dependency on KIMI-CLONE-PROVEN is mentioned but not defined here.
notes: A design spec for a cross-scheme territory composer that is deliberately guarded so context gathering never kills a turn, currently held pending host/consumer gating.
questions: If the status is BUILT + VERIFIED-BY-USE, what exactly does 'BUILD HELD' mean for runtime/territory.py?
Which 9 schemes and which 6 non-resolvable schemes are definitive, or are the listed categories only representative?
What is the precise content of the proposed bridge handoff patch file?
How is the KIMI-CLONE-PROVEN gating evaluated and by whom?
When both detail.source and detail.record are present for a corpus point, which is preferred and why?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is a design document for a held build; actual content also acts as a post-build verification log and refinement journal, mixing design, implementation status, and corrected live-store findings.
apparent_themes: Fail-soft degrade contracts to preserve downstream wire behavior.
Brain-agnostic separation of territory composition from focus/panel handoff.
Meet-at-the-address / file-disjoint collaboration between module owners.
Verify-by-use against live store rather than relying on assumptions.
intention_signals: It is trying to define a safe, general-purpose address context composer that can replace a ui://-only inline composer in the bridge, while remaining held until downstream consumers and gating conditions are ready.
novelty: The explicit degrade matrix and per-leg guarding for a cross-scheme composer, plus the live-store correction that turned a guessed code:// scope leg into a corpus-record dereference path.
contribution: A guarded, scheme-dispatching territory composition contract and a concrete degrade matrix that a fused system could adopt to make address context gathering robust across resolvable and non-resolvable schemes.
summary_for_embedding: Design document for territory_for, a brain-agnostic address-to-territory composer with per-leg degrade guards, a bridge handoff plan, and live-store-verified corpus resolution refinements.
intention_for_embedding: To specify a drop-in replacement for the bridge's inline ui:// context composer that safely composes identity, context, chats, and relations for any address scheme.
